completion_rule: A deliverable is complete when its implementation, deterministic local evidence, and rollback strategy are present. Provider-native activation is a separate gate and is never inferred from fixtures or local CLI probes.

deliverables.rollback: Revert the runtime ADR/manifest change and rerun the product-truth verifier.
Restore the previous manifest and regenerate only derived truth documents.
Disable the opt-in smoke environment and retain deterministic runtime tests.
Reject incompatible reports and restore the previous versioned evaluator contract.
Revert claim wording only after the product-truth tests accept the replacement.
Remove gateway wiring while keeping the canonical MCP runtime operational.
Restore direct registered-tool dispatch and rerun the gateway regression suite.
Disable cache/offload hooks and return bounded direct tool results.
Disable model invocation when reservations cannot be enforced; never bypass budgets.
Restore the last valid state snapshot and retain fail-closed writes.
Restore the frozen corpus fingerprint and reject drifted reports.
Keep adaptive routing disabled and discard non-native receipts.
Fail closed to the strong-model route when canary gates lack valid evidence.
Disable rollout and restore the last accepted provider-native policy snapshot.
Disable paid review routing when avoided-defect evidence does not exceed cost.
Stop release locally on any aggregate-gate failure; no hosted workflow is required.
Run the rollback rehearsal and do not publish artifacts that fail policy verification.
Remove generated project metadata or restore preserved pre-existing files.
Downgrade unsupported maturity labels and rerun every declared verification command.
